expectations.evidence: Step 5 (Diagnose) appears before Step 6 (Implement the Fix) and explicitly states: 'load_settings() is being called before super().__init__(master). Since the Frame base class hasn't been initialized yet, self is not a valid tkinter widget.'
The response invokes the screenshot skill twice: 'python3 ~/.claude/skills/screenshot-eval/scripts/screenshot.py --window "Settings"' in Step 3 and again in Step 7 after the fix.
The fix is described as 'One line reordered, minimal diff' — only the order of super().__init__(master) and self.load_settings() is swapped, with no broader refactoring.
Step 7 explicitly kills the app and relaunches: 'pkill -f "app.py" 2>/dev/null; sleep 1 / python3 app.py &' followed by a re-screenshot.

notes: All four assertions passed cleanly. The response follows the expected gui-debugger workflow: screenshot before diagnosis, explicit root cause, minimal fix, relaunch and re-screenshot. The fix description is accurate (super().__init__ must come first).
